title.rendered: Pokémon Day Out: Brooklyn Bridge Park

acf.time_notes: Pokémon Day Out: Brooklyn Bridge Park runs July 24–July 26
acf.recurring_events_list:
Pokémon Day Out: Brooklyn Bridge Park – July 25 (July 25, 2026)
Pokémon Day Out: Brooklyn Bridge Park – July 26 (July 26, 2026)
acf.description:
Pokémon Day Out is a live, family-friendly experience where families can take part in an unforgettable Pokémon celebration. During your visit, you’ll set off on a journey filled with playful moments, joyful discoveries, and opportunities to discover your favorite Pokémon along the way. Games, activities and plenty of surprises are in store as fans of all ages gather to celebrate 30 years of Pokémon together.

Pokémon Day Out is free to attend, but advance reservations are required. A parent/guardian who is 18+ must register on behalf of themselves and any minors aged 18 or below.

Learn More!

Due to extreme temperatures this event has been cancelled – our Education Center will be open with extended hours 10-6pm on Wednesday, July 1–Friday, July 3. Drop-in, cool off, have some fun & learn!

Join us to learn about the vital role oysters play in the New York estuary and get some hands on experience in oyster monitoring.

Register Now!

Accessibility: Please reach out to education@bbp.nyc for any accessibility needs or questions. The paths on the pier are paved, and the workshop will take place at the picnic tables where seating is available. Bathrooms are located nearby at Fornino’s on Pier 6.

title.rendered: Storytime with Brooklyn Public Library – July 31

acf.recurring_events_list:
Storytime with Brooklyn Public Library – June 19 (June 19, 2026)

Storytime with Brooklyn Public Library – June 26 (June 26, 2026)

Storytime with Brooklyn Public Library – July 3 (July 3, 2026)

Storytime with Brooklyn Public Library – July 10 (July 10, 2026)

Storytime with Brooklyn Public Library – July 17 (July 17, 2026)

Storytime with Brooklyn Public Library – July 24 (July 24, 2026)

Storytime with Brooklyn Public Library – July 31 (July 31, 2026)
acf.description:
Calling all caregivers and young children – join for Storytime with Brooklyn Public Library on Friday afternoons in June and July!

Librarians from Brooklyn Public Library’s Adams St and Brooklyn Heights branches will join to lead storytime each week. Bring a blanket, some snacks, and enjoy a relaxing late afternoon in the shade of Pier 1’s The Vale Lawn.

Please note there is no storytime on June 19 or July 3

Don’t miss the dance party of the season, as DJ collective Papi Juice return to Pier 6 Plaza as guest curators.

Set against the waterfront skyline at Pier 6 Plaza, come together for music, movement, and community. After lighting up the park the past few summers, Papi Juice is back with their signature energy, vibrant DJs, and joyful atmosphere – join to dance together under the open sky, watch a beautiful sunset, and create some magic!

DJs for the evening are Papi Juice’s own Oscar Nñ & Adam R., Precious Renee Tucker, and Soul Summit. 

Guests are welcome to bring food and non-alcoholic beverages to picnic (no glass or outside alcohol allowed). Snacks, soft drinks, and alcoholic beverages will be available for purchase at our on-site bar by Fornino.

Register Now!

title.rendered: Movement for Healthy Aging with Mark Morris Dance Group: Restorative Movement

acf.description:
Co-presented by Brooklyn Bridge Park and Mark Morris Dance Group

Join Mark Morris Dance Group for an interactive dance workshop, exploring accessible movement and wellness. Designed for those with limited mobility, this workshop is focused on flexibility, balance, and range of motion. No prior dance experience required—just bring your energy and favorite dance attire! All ages welcome.

On Sunday, September 27, join for Restorative Movement, a somatic movement class with a focus on mobility, flexibility, balance, and range of motion at Pier 6, Liberty Lawn.

Space is limited; pre-registration is required.

Register Now!

Family Kayaking – August 30 (August 30, 2026)
acf.description:
Glide along the water this summer and fall in our protected embayment with free public kayaking!

Presented in partnership with Brooklyn Bridge Park Boathouse, free kayaking is offered throughout the summer on Wednesday and Thursday evenings, Saturdays, and Sunday afternoons. All levels are welcome including first-time kayakers — life vests and all equipment provided, as well as instruction. Children under 18 must have an adult guardian present. Meet at the north entrance of Pier 2, look for the floating dock.

Our Sunday program is specifically geared for kids and families, allowing younger paddlers to go solo in single kayaks! Kids aged 7 and older can paddle solo (as long as a guardian is present) thanks to additional on-water safety boaters from The Kayak Foundation who are specially trained to work with kids.

Advanced registration highly recommended, providing 20 minute timed slots. Registration opens 2 weeks ahead of each date through the Brooklyn Bridge Park Boathouse website. View all kayaking dates here. Occasionally, walk-up spots may be available, but not guaranteed. Please note that holiday weekends may be skipped; please check the schedule.

Register here!

Don’t see any available time slots? Check back! Registration opens on a rolling basis, exactly 2 weeks ahead of each date. Occasionally, slots may open due to cancellations or no-shows.

Have your own boat? Launch your human-powered boats from the Pier 4 Beach. Learn more.

title.rendered: Movies With A View: Bend It Like Beckham

acf.description:
One of Brooklyn Bridge Park’s most popular series, Movies With A View presented by Persol, returns for its 26th season with outdoor films on Thursday evenings in July and August – expanding with an additional family film night on September 18.

Leading up to the World Cup Final, revisit the popular film Bend It Like Beckham (PG-13, 2002, 112 minutes), about a young woman chasing her passion for soccer despite her family’s wishes.

Come early to enjoy a picnic on the lawn and refreshing waterfront breezes, with New York Harbor and Manhattan skyline views like no other as the sun sets. Entry is free and first-come, first serve starting at 6:00 PM. Movies begin at sunset.

Guests are welcome to bring their own food for picnicking and encouraged to support nearby Park concessions and neighborhood restaurants. Popcorn, water, soft drinks, and alcoholic beverages (for those 21+) will be available for sale on-site by Fornino. Attendees are encouraged to bring picnic blankets or small soft cushions but reminded that lawn chairs and dogs are not permitted on the lawn, and glass bottles and outside alcohol are never allowed in the Park.

acf.time_notes: Weekly on Saturday mornings, June through August

Youth Basketball Clinics – August 29 (August 29, 2026)
acf.description:
Kids aged 6-17 can join Brooklyn Bridge Park for free basketball clinics led by KING Hoops, NYC’s premier youth basketball organization. Participants can learn basketball basics as head coaches lead participants through drills and games. Free clinics are held on Saturday mornings from June through August (excluding holiday weekends).

The first hour, 9-10am, will be for beginner players who are brand new to the sport. Here we will teach the very basic fundamentals of the game, the rules of the game, and getting acclimated to having a ball in each player’s hands.

The second hour, 10-11am, will be for advanced beginners to intermediate players who have played a little bit more basketball experience. Emphasis will be on ball handling, passing, shooting form, defense, and learning to love the game.

This event is rain or shine. It is recommended to bring your own ball; we will have a limited number available to borrow onsite.

Advanced registration and fitness waivers are required, although walk-up registrations can be accommodated as space allows. Registration is by-the-week and dates open at start of the month. Capacity will be limited to 45 participants per age group.

Register Now!

Volunteer Orientation – May 14 (May 14, 2026)

Volunteer Orientation – May 27 (May 27, 2026)

Volunteer Orientation – June 9 (June 9, 2026)
acf.description:
Volunteer Orientations are a great way to learn about available opportunities at Brooklyn Bridge Park. During these in-person sessions, prospective volunteers will learn about the history of the Park, meet Park staff, and explore the many ways to get involved. 

Volunteer Orientations are required for all volunteer roles except for Green Teams and Coastal Cleanups. 

While all ages are welcome, the session will focus primarily on opportunities for volunteers aged 18 and over. 

To register for an orientation: 

  • If you’re brand new to volunteering at Brooklyn Bridge Park, create a volunteer profile to sign-up for an Orientation  
  • If you’ve already got a Volunteer Profile, sign-in to and navigate to your Volunteer Calendar register for an upcoming Orientation  

The exact meeting location will be shared with registered participants a few days in advance. 

Learn more about volunteer opportunities here.
